chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.1085 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs 1. For orders on M.A. No.5941/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objection a/w reply as at “A” 3. For orders on M.A. No.5942/2025 (Exp/A) 4. For hearing of bail application ------------------------- 28.04.2025 Mr. Muhammad Asif Malik, advocate for applicants ------------------------------------ Applicants/accused Ashfaq Ahmed son of Abdul Rehman, Mehrab son of Abdul Jabbar and Danish Riaz Minhas son of Muhammad Riaz Minhas, seek protective bail pre-arrest bail in FIR No.467/2024, registered at P.S. Khawaja Ajmair Nagri for offence under sections 324, 427, 506-B, 34, PPC read with Section 25-D of the Telegraph Act, 1885. Learned counsel for applicant submits that applicants are innocent and they have been falsely implicated in this case due to mala fide intentions and ulterior motives; that applicants/accused are law abiding citizens and they intend to surrender before the concerned Court but they feel apprehension of their arrest at the hands of police in order to humiliate and disgrace them, hence they need some protection to enable them to appear before the Court concerned. Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicants/accused above named, for a period of five days from today, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees twenty five thousand) each and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicants/accused are directed to surrender before the trial Court concerned in the aforesaid period and join the investigation/trial forthwith.

Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicants/accused above named, for a period of five days from today, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees twenty five thousand) each and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicants/accused are directed to surrender before the trial Court concerned in the aforesaid period and join the investigation/trial forthwith. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of five days from today or whenever applicants/accused surrender before the trial Court, which one occurs earlier. It is made clear, in case, applicants/accused fail to surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period, their bail bonds shall stand automatically forfeited. The instant criminal bail application is accordingly disposed of. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
